title: No test demonstrates that field-put drops other fields
description: The 'Partial-update footgun' warning in the module docs is accurate. But no test demonstrates the actual behavior: that put with ?field=a on a secret already containing {a:x, b:y} produces a new version containing only {a: new_value}, not {a: new_value, b: y}. Without a test, the warning looks like defensive documentation rather than an observed fact. A future contributor might assume the docs are overly cautious and 'simplify' the code by adding a read-modify-write. Suggested addition: integration test that writes two fields, then overwrites one with ?field=, then reads the full JSON and asserts the other field is absent.
